purpose.summary: The COPAUA0C program writes authorization details to a database and closes the request queue. It updates a summary record with approved/declined authorization counts and amounts, inserts detailed authorization information, and closes the MQSeries request queue if it is open. The program handles IMS database updates and MQSeries queue closures, logging errors if necessary.
purpose.business_context: This program is likely part of an authorization processing system, where it records authorization requests and responses for accounting and auditing purposes.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: Summary of pending authorizations for an account.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Detailed information about a specific authorization request.

inputs.name: ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT
inputs.description: Cash credit limit for the account.

inputs.name: PA-TRANSACTION-AMT
inputs.description: Transaction amount from the pending authorization.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-*
inputs.description: Various fields from the authorization request (date, time, card number, etc.).

inputs.name: PA-RL-*
inputs.description: Various fields from the authorization response (ID code, response code, reason, approved amount).

inputs.name: W01-HCONN-REQUEST
inputs.description: MQSeries connection handle for the request queue.

inputs.name: W01-HOBJ-REQUEST
inputs.description: MQSeries object handle for the request queue.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
outputs.description: Updated summary of pending authorizations, including counts and amounts of approved and declined authorizations.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
outputs.description: Detailed record of the authorization request and response.

outputs.name: AUDIT-LOG
outputs.description: Error log containing information about failures during IMS updates or MQSeries queue closures.  Implied by PERFORM 9500-LOG-ERROR.

business_rules.description: If the authorization response is approved, increment the approved authorization count and amount in the pending authorization summary.
business_rules.logic_summary: Adds 1 to PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT and WS-APPROVED-AMT to PA-APPROVED-AUTH-AMT.  Also adds WS-APPROVED-AMT to PA-CREDIT-BALANCE and sets PA-CASH-BALANCE to 0.

business_rules.description: If the authorization response is declined, increment the declined authorization count and amount in the pending authorization summary.
business_rules.logic_summary: Adds 1 to PA-DECLINED-AUTH-CNT and PA-TRANSACTION-AMT to PA-DECLINED-AUTH-AMT.

business_rules.description: Sets PA-MATCH-PENDING to TRUE if the authorization response is approved, otherwise sets PA-MATCH-AUTH-DECLINED to TRUE.
business_rules.logic_summary: Uses SET verb based on AUTH-RESP-APPROVED condition.

transforms.transformation_description: Extracts the year and day from the current date.
Moves the current time to a numeric field.
Calculates the current time with milliseconds.
Calculates the authorization date by subtracting the year and day from 99999.
Calculates the authorization time by subtracting the time with milliseconds from 999999999.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the Pending Authorization Summary segment in IMS.
Defines the structure of the Pending Authorization Detail segment in IMS.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph serves as a control point for writing authorization information to the database. It orchestrates the update of the pending authorization summary and the insertion of the authorization details. It first performs 8400-UPDATE-SUMMARY to update the summary record in the IMS database with information about the approved or declined authorization. Then, it performs 8500-INSERT-AUTH to insert the detailed authorization information into the IMS database. This paragraph does not directly handle any data manipulation or error conditions; it delegates these tasks to the called paragraphs. The paragraph ensures that both the summary and detail records are updated for each authorization processed.

This paragraph provides a standard exit point for the 8000-WRITE-AUTH-TO-DB paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. This paragraph does not perform any processing or data manipulation. It serves solely as a structural element to facilitate the PERFORM THRU construct used in the calling paragraph. This ensures a clean and controlled exit from the 8000-WRITE-AUTH-TO-DB process.

This paragraph updates the pending authorization summary in the IMS database. It first checks if a summary record already exists for the account. If not (NFOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG is true), it initializes the PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY, moves the account and customer IDs from XREF-ACCT-ID and XREF-CUST-ID to the summary record. It then moves the account credit limits to the summary record. Based on whether the authorization was approved or declined (AUTH-RESP-APPROVED), it increments the appropriate counters and amounts in the summary record. Finally, it either updates the existing summary record (FOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG) or inserts a new one into the IMS database using EXEC DLI REPL or ISRT respectively. If the IMS update fails, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR.

This paragraph provides a standard exit point for the 8400-UPDATE-SUMMARY paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. This paragraph does not perform any processing or data manipulation. It serves solely as a structural element to facilitate the PERFORM THRU construct used in the calling paragraph. This ensures a clean and controlled exit from the 8400-UPDATE-SUMMARY process.

This paragraph inserts detailed authorization information into the IMS database. It first retrieves the current date and time using CICS ASKTIME and FORMATTIME commands, converting them into specific formats for storage. It then moves data from the authorization request (PA-RQ-*) and response (PA-RL-*) fields into the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS record. Based on whether the authorization was approved or declined (AUTH-RESP-APPROVED), it sets either PA-MATCH-PENDING or PA-MATCH-AUTH-DECLINED to TRUE. It then inserts the detailed authorization record into the IMS database using EXEC DLI ISRT. If the IMS insert fails, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR.

This paragraph provides a standard exit point for the 8500-INSERT-AUTH paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. This paragraph does not perform any processing or data manipulation. It serves solely as a structural element to facilitate the PERFORM THRU construct used in the calling paragraph. This ensures a clean and controlled exit from the 8500-INSERT-AUTH process.

This paragraph handles the termination of the program. It first checks if the IMS PSB has been scheduled (IMS-PSB-SCHD). If so, it terminates the IMS PSB using EXEC DLI TERM. It then performs 9100-CLOSE-REQUEST-QUEUE to close the MQSeries request queue. This paragraph ensures that both the IMS connection and the MQSeries queue are properly closed before the program ends. The paragraph does not directly handle any data manipulation; it delegates the queue closure to the 9100-CLOSE-REQUEST-QUEUE paragraph.

This paragraph provides a standard exit point for the 9000-TERMINATE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. This paragraph does not perform any processing or data manipulation. It serves solely as a structural element to facilitate the PERFORM THRU construct used in the calling paragraph. This ensures a clean and controlled exit from the 9000-TERMINATE process.

This paragraph closes the MQSeries request queue if it is open. It checks if the queue is open (WS-REQUEST-MQ-OPEN). If so, it calls the MQCLOSE program to close the queue, passing the connection handle (W01-HCONN-REQUEST), object handle (W01-HOBJ-REQUEST), and other necessary parameters. After the MQCLOSE call, it checks the completion code (WS-COMPCODE). If the closure was successful (MQCC-OK), it sets WS-REQUEST-MQ-CLSE to TRUE. Otherwise, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR, including the completion code and reason code from the MQCLOSE call.

This paragraph provides a standard exit point for the 9100-CLOSE-REQUEST-QUEUE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. This paragraph does not perform any processing or data manipulation. It serves solely as a structural element to facilitate the PERFORM THRU construct used in the calling paragraph. This ensures a clean and controlled exit from the 9100-CLOSE-REQUEST-QUEUE process.

error_handling.condition: IMS Update Summary Failed (DIBSTAT not OK)
error_handling.action: Move 'I003' to ERR-LOCATION, set ERR-CRITICAL and ERR-IMS to TRUE, move IMS-RETURN-CODE to ERR-CODE-1, move 'IMS UPDATE SUMRY FAILED' to ERR-MESSAGE, move PA-CARD-NUM to ERR-EVENT-KEY, and perform 9500-LOG-ERROR.

error_handling.condition: IMS Insert Detail Failed (DIBSTAT not OK)
error_handling.action: Move 'I004' to ERR-LOCATION, set ERR-CRITICAL and ERR-IMS to TRUE, move IMS-RETURN-CODE to ERR-CODE-1, move 'IMS INSERT DETL FAILED' to ERR-MESSAGE, move PA-CARD-NUM to ERR-EVENT-KEY, and perform 9500-LOG-ERROR.

error_handling.condition: Failed to close request MQ (WS-COMPCODE not MQCC-OK)
error_handling.action: Move 'M005' to ERR-LOCATION, set ERR-WARNING and ERR-MQ to TRUE, move WS-COMPCODE and WS-REASON to ERR-CODE-1 and ERR-CODE-2 respectively, move 'FAILED TO CLOSE REQUEST MQ' to ERR-MESSAGE, and perform 9500-LOG-ERROR.

cics_operations.purpose: Formats the absolute time into year, day, time, and milliseconds.
